title: Fixed base path variable
body:
## Description

This pr solves the issues with the base uri path. In React Scripts everything depended from a so called base tag. This base tag was rewritten when serving the frontend leading to the JavaScript and CSS being loaded from the rewritten tag in the Java code.  This pr simply takes all resource links and points them to a new location that is set via the properties.


## What does this pr not solve

During my testing I realized that you still need a proxy that rewrites the paths and fetches them from the original location. Only the paths in the index.html are rewritten. So the resource location needs to be configured via proxy config but this seems to be also the case before.


Closes #3742
author: SamTV12345

linked_issues.title: ZIPKIN_UI_BASEPATH doesn't seem to work with 3.1.0
linked_issues.body:
We have upgraded to 3.1.0. We are using `ZIPKIN_UI_BASEPATH` to rewrite the base path for the UI, but it doesn't seem to work anymore. Tried running:

```
docker run -p 9411:9411 openzipkin/zipkin:3.1.0
```

and the UI shows nicely on http://localhost:9411 (redirects to `http://localhost:9411/zipkin/`). With 

```
docker run -e ZIPKIN_UI_BASEPATH=/admin/zipkin -p 9411:9411 openzipkin/zipkin:3.1.0
```

it still redirects to `http://localhost:9411/zipkin/` but shows blank page. If I try `localhost:9411/admin/zipkin/` I get `404 Not Found`.

Perhaps I am missing some configuration changes for the upgrade?

title: [Improvement-16754][DataX] Support DataX writer parameter batchSize
body: ## Was this PR generated or assisted by AI? YES — used Claude (Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.7) to help review the original design, simplify the implementation (removing per-database dynamic defaults that were the wrong abstraction), and resolve rebase conflicts against `dev`. All design decisions, the final approach, and verification were reviewed by me. ## Purpose of the pull request DataX writer currently does not expose the `batchSize` parameter, so users are stuck with the DataX default of `2048`. This is too small for some target databases — e.g. the ClickHouse plugin recommends `65536`. This PR makes `batchSize` configurable from the task UI. Closes #16754 ## Brief change log Backend (`dolphinscheduler-task-datax`): - Add `batchSize` field to `DataxParameters` (lombok `@Data` generates accessors) - In `DataxTask`, write `batchSize` into the writer JSON only when value > 0, so the DataX upstream default still applies when the user picks `0 (unlimited)` - Extend `DataxParametersTest` to cover the new field Frontend (`dolphinscheduler-ui`): - Add a `batchSize` select control on the DataX task form - Static option list: `0 / 1024 / 2048 / 4096 / 8192 / 16384 / 32768 / 65536 / 131072` - Default `2048` (matches DataX upstream); ClickHouse / Databend users can pick `65536` / `131072` from the same list - Add `zh_CN` and `en_US` i18n entries (`datax_writer_batch_size`) **Design note**: an earlier draft switched the option list and default based on target database type (see [issue discussion](https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues/16754#issuecomment-4319892995)). That was dropped after review — `batchSize` optima depend on row width, network, and target hardware, not just on the database, so hard-coding database-specific defaults in the UI is the wrong abstraction. A static list keeps the UI consistent with surrounding fields (`jobSpeedByte` / `jobSpeedRecord` / `memoryLimit`). ## Verify this pull request This change added tests and can be verified as follows: - Added `testBatchSize` in `DataxParametersTest` covering the new field (default `0`, `2048`, `65536`) - Updated the existing `toString` assertion in `DataxParametersTest` to include `batchSize=0` - Manually verified locally: - Created a DataX task targeting MySQL with `batchSize=2048` — generated JSON contains `"batchSize": 2048` under `writer.parameter` - Set `batchSize=0` — generated JSON omits the field, DataX falls back to its own default - Switched UI between values, persisted task, reloaded — value preserved ## Screenshots ## Pull Request Notice [Pull Request Notice](https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/blob/dev/docs/docs/en/contribute/join/pull-request.md)
author: leocook

title: fix(admin): guard empty evaluator model config
body:
Fixes #4743

Summary: Guard the evaluator list's model badge renderer against empty or malformed `modelConfig` values so partially configured evaluators render as `-` instead of crashing the whole page with `"undefined" is not valid JSON`.

Validation: `npm run build:flow` and `npm run build:app` both passed in `spring-ai-alibaba-admin/frontend`. The evaluator list now safely treats missing or invalid `modelConfig` as an unconfigured evaluator state.

title: Replace language server WebSocket connection with Ydoc
body:
### Pull Request Description

<!--
- Please describe the nature of your PR here, as well as the motivation for it.
- If it fixes an open issue, please mention that issue number here.
-->

- close #14142

PR replaces binary and JSON-RPC WebSockets with Yjs based communication.

```
+------------------+                +--------------------------------------------------------------+
|                  |                |                                                              |
| +--------------+ |    WebSocket   | +-----------------+   YjsChannel.Server  +-----------------+ |
| |  IDE Client  | |<-------------->| |  Ydoc Server    |<-------------------->| Language Server | |
| +--------------+ |                | +-----------------+                      +-----------------+ |
|     Electron     |                |                         GraalVM                              |
+------------------+                +--------------------------------------------------------------+
```

Workflow:
- IDE client creates a `YjsChannel` backed by Yjs `Y.Array` allowing bidirectional message passing
- IDE client connects to the Ydoc server using the [Yjs WebSocket provider](https://docs.yjs.dev/ecosystem/connection-provider/y-websocket)
- Ydoc server creates a corresponding `YjsChannel` that is synchronized with the client's channel and passes it to the Language server via `YjsChannelCallbacks` callback

This way IDE client and the Language Server establish `YjsChannel` connection and start exchanging messages.

linked_issues.title: Avoid networking layer of Akka by `ydoc-server` talking directly to the engine+ls
linked_issues.body:
### Related Prior Work
 
- now when #13178 has been integrated
- #5419 was dreaming about:
  - using Helidon to implement network communication
  - writing Language Server in JavaScript
- since #10783 & co. Helidon can be used for `WebSocket` emulation
- discussion about [using Y.js to simulate duplex network communication](https://discord.com/channels/401396655599124480/1425766988839653377/1425770149683789946)

### Task 1: Avoid two WebSocket Connections

- modify IDE & `ydoc-shared` code
- avoid IDE creating two `WebSocket` connections (one to `ydoc-server` another to language server_)
- let IDE connect to `ydoc-server` only and ...
- ... "send messages" by adding them to FIFO Y.js queue
- let the `ydoc-server` distribute these messages to language server
- sketch of necessary changes: [Connect MainModule.scala and languageServer.ts so they can all to each other](https://github.com/enso-org/enso/compare/develop...JaroslavTulach:enso:YdocDirect)

### Task 2: Ydoc Server Talks to Language Server Directly

- with #13178 the Ydoc server no longer has to send network messages
- but it can make direct calls into _language server_
- by doing so we can eliminate the _network Akka_ layer

### Future Work: Less Messages

- as @farmaazon puts it:
- _we could do even better. Many endpoints are for altering some state managed by LS (execution context, file contents...). We should just update structures representing that state._
- all that's going to be enabled once we finish tasks 1 and 2

linked_issues.body:
Docs https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/howtos/generate-client-libraries/09renaming/#preserving-exact-casing
A new exact() function that preserves client names without language-specific casing transformations. Previously, names provided via @clientName would still be subject to emitter-specific casing rules (e.g., PascalCase for C#, snake_case for Python). Now you can opt out of those transformations entirely.

Java emitter should support this function.

- [X] I had searched in the [issues](https://github.com/apache/incubator-streampark/issues?q=is%3Aissue+label%3A%22bug%22) and found no similar issues.


### Java Version

1.8

### Scala Version

2.11.x

### StreamPark Version

2.1.4

### Flink Version

1.18.1

### deploy mode

yarn-application

### What happened

调用接口：/flink/app/start

1. 在令牌管理页面使用admin用户生成令牌后，在 t_access_token 表中拿到令牌然后调用接口
2. 调用接口是定时任务，在上午能成功启动任务运行完成，过了3小时后，没做任何修改，再次运行时接口返回401
3. 验证token 是一致的
进入作业详情 复制作业的启动curl ：

curl -X POST '/flink/app/start' \
-H 'Authorization: 69qMW7reOXhrAh29LjPWwwP+quFqLf++MbPbsB9/NcTCKGzZE2EU7tBUBU5gqG236VF5pMyVrsE5K7hBWiyuLqICNwPEEj7zzjFzA1ji5V6Hj3O3o2/ZwIYuIdSdSedz01hOX8wwQTJvbbp8kbAEenDllTnlkTxKB910HXaUKUxRhqUDubDlxsn3KBDrYO43WaUoxZBOlEwmc1Mgc5EWpXRclTPOFwQnPKrFL4YXgWU=' \
-H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded; charset=UTF-8' \
--data-urlencode 'teamId=100000' \
--data-urlencode 'allowNonRestored=false' \
--data-urlencode 'id=100005' \
--data-urlencode 'savePointed=false' \
-i
进行数据库查询 t_access_token 表中的token，对比发现后是一致的，确定调用 的接口也是该表中的token字段没有任何调整，在次手动触发一次，依旧返回401，使用curl执行也是返回401
4.页面F12排查
通过页面F12发现，接口调用的已经是另一个token了
页面的token：69qMW7reOXhrAh29LjPWwwP+quFqLf++MbPbsB9/NcTCKGzZE2EU7tBUBU5gqG236VF5pMyVrsE5K7hBWiyuLuuoqIY1ywErlAdu3x+j4qxsjTP05Zy79vbouIGBZun34J55n8fuKwdwFJadsMG82x0Ldejm5Gp0qgImkwLo2CxSTkMBcBSADuDMNaYIerR1HqMeAFhy3TgMq16Z8rAz7pgXO33ZZNcQNADllg07uIY=

然后将页面的新token替换接口参数去执行，能正常执行，作业运行完成
页面的token是不固定的，所以也不能直接使用，只能测试验证对比


### Error Exception

```log
调用接口提示的日志：
2024-08-01 15:38:44 | INFO  | XNIO-1 task-5 | org.apache.streampark.console.base.handler.GlobalExceptionHandler:54] Unauthenticated: This subject is anonymous - it does not have any identifying principals and authorization operations require an identity to check against.  A Subject instance will acquire these identifying principals automatically after a successful login is performed be executing org.apache.shiro.subj

title: http-client-java, support exact in clientName
body:
fix https://github.com/Azure/autorest.java/issues/3340

manual tested on https://github.com/Azure/typespec-azure/blob/main/packages/azure-http-specs/specs/azure/client-generator-core/exact-name/main.tsp
need its release to write e2e

---

Impl is relatively simple. If `isExactName`, set the name directly to `language.java.name`. And later in Java code this name will not get transformed (otherwise, Java code will do a transform from `language.default.name` to `language.java.name`).

---

It is not complete though. There is `isExactName` missing from some types. TCGC fixed but not released.
https://github.com/Azure/typespec-azure/pull/4480
author: weidongxu-microsoft

linked_issues.title: Support exact() Function for @clientName (TCGC 0.68.0)
linked_issues.body:
Docs https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/howtos/generate-client-libraries/09renaming/#preserving-exact-casing
A new exact() function that preserves client names without language-specific casing transformations. Previously, names provided via @clientName would still be subject to emitter-specific casing rules (e.g., PascalCase for C#, snake_case for Python). Now you can opt out of those transformations entirely.

Java emitter should support this function.

linked_issues.body:
### Description

Deprecate the old way of using safe area insets (custom vars) and use the native approach instead

### Platforms

- [ ] iOS
- [ ] Android
- [ ] Web

### Request or proposed solution

Would you be willing to do this? We've discussed this before. For example [here](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/pull/8268#issuecomment-3613142117) and [here](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/pull/8180#issuecomment-3520458761).

The current approach taken by Capacitor is quite different than the native approach and to me it seems inferior.  I've outlined the differences [here](https://github.com/capacitor-community/safe-area?tab=readme-ov-file#differences-between-this-plugin-system-bars-and-status-bar).

The main reason you took this approach, was

> For us, the issue isn’t spec compliance — it’s whether this is reliable enough to serve as a runtime contract across OEMs, OS versions, etc.

However, as far as I can see, it seems, the current approach achieves quite the opposite. There were a few bugs that needed to be fixed (while it already was marked as production-ready) and there are still other open bugs ([ex. 1](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/issues/8329), [ex. 2](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/issues/8287)).

What's also bothering me, is that the code has a few quirks.

- The code doesn't bother for Android versions prior v15, which makes older Android versions just break without a developer directly noticing ([source](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/pull/8268#discussion_r2588782642))
  - <sup>This seems to contradict the reason why you chose to go this route btw</sup>
- When installing Capacitor v8 fresh, a developer would never know (without reading the docs thoroughly) that his app is broking on Chromium versions < M140 ([source](https://github.com/ionic-team/capacitor/pull/8268#discussion_r2605888892))

linked_issues.title: `sdpMid` and `sdpMLineIndex` are  both required although they are optional/nullable in the spec
linked_issues.body:
#### Expected Behavior:
`RTCIceCandidate` should allow initiation with either `sdpMid`,  or `sdpMLineIndex`
Spec defines them as nullable:
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/RTCIceCandidate#instance_properties

Only when both `sdpMid` &  `sdpMLineIndex` are null should an error be raised:
![image](https://github.com/react-native-webrtc/react-native-webrtc/assets/4996164/86e30994-f80c-4e3e-bb76-4974843393d8)

#### Observed Behavior:
When trying to connect to a signaling server which doesn't send 'sdpMid', but does send `sdpMLineIndex` as part of the ice candidate message, there is the following error:
```
cannot parse incoming message from signaling server [TypeError: `sdpMLineIndex` and `sdpMid` must not null]
```

#### Steps to reproduce the issue:
1) Connect to a signaling server not necessarily sending 'sdpMid' field, such as gstreamer webrtcsink's signaling server
2) See the issue

#### Platform Information  
* **React Native Version**: React Native 0.73.1
* **WebRTC Module Version**: 118.0.2

Changes compared with the points he mentioned in his PR:

title: Fix memory leaks & crashes when loading multiple Go extensions in one Python process
body:
Relates-to: https://github.com/go-python/gopy/issues/392 
Fixes: https://github.com/go-python/gopy/issues/385
Fixes: https://github.com/go-python/gopy/issues/370

---

I took [Scusemua](https://github.com/Scusemua)'s two commits in https://github.com/go-python/gopy/pull/361 as a base and built a broader set of fixes on top, covering memory leaks, GC coordination, and shared library conflicts.

This PR wouldn't exist without the work Scusemua put in first. His two commits from July 2024 fixed a subtle but nasty bug: Go code was calling a Python function without holding the GIL.

That's a precise fix that prevents several hard-to-reproduce crashes, and everything else here builds on top of it.

---

I added four fixes in the same area:

1. C string memory leaks. Generated code allocated C strings when reading string fields from Go structs, slices, and maps, but never freed them. Switched those paths to a helper that frees after Python copies the value.

2. Unsafe GC call. The generated wrapper was calling Go's GC from a thread that crosses the C/Go boundary, which could panic on multi-core machines. Rerouted it through a background goroutine via a channel.

3. GC out of sync. Without coordination, Go heap objects piled up between Python GC cycles. Fixed by registering a gc.callbacks handler that triggers a Go collection after each Python one.

4. Symbol conflicts between co-loaded extensions. Loading two gopy-built shared libraries with RTLD_GLOBAL caused one library's Go internals to clobber the other's, corrupting heap state. Fixed by loading without that flag so each library's runtime stays isolated.

5. I added a regression test for two extensions in a single Python process, stealing from the discussion in issue 370. 

I also expanded the CI matrix to cover more Go versions and macOS configs.

title: fix(harness): stream output with idle watchdog and explicit stdin null (#695)
body:
Fixes #695

## Problem

The CLI harness runner in all three SDKs blocked until the child process fully exited and read its output only at the end. Because it never streamed stdout, it could not apply a no-progress (idle) watchdog. When an opencode provider call stalled (commonly a stalled OpenRouter streaming response), the runner could not tell "stuck" from "working" and waited up to the wall-clock cap (default 1800s), freezing the run and holding a concurrency-semaphore slot the whole time. The TypeScript runner additionally opened stdin as a pipe that was never closed, so a child that probes stdin could hang waiting for EOF.

## Fix per language

Same shape in all three SDKs. Return types and full stdout/stderr strings are unchanged, so downstream JSONL parsing is unaffected. The wall-clock timeout is kept as the outer bound.

Python (`sdk/python/agentfield/harness/_cli.py`):
- Stream stdout and stderr concurrently into separate buffers, tracking the timestamp of the last received chunk.
- If no output arrives for `idle_seconds` (resolved from the `idle_seconds` arg, then env `AGENTFIELD_HARNESS_IDLE_SECONDS`, then 120s; a value <= 0 disables it), kill the process group and raise `TimeoutError`.
- Set `stdin=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL` and `start_new_session=True` so the child gets an immediate stdin EOF and its own process group.

Go (`sdk/go/harness/cli.go`, plus `cli_unix.go` and `cli_windows.go`):
- Replace `c.Run()` with in-memory buffers by `StdoutPipe`/`StderrPipe` drained in two goroutines, guarded by a mutex, recording last-activity.
- A one-second ticker checks the idle window and kills the process group when it is exceeded, returning a no-progress error.
- Set `c.Stdin` to an empty reader and run the child in its own process group (`Setpgid` on Unix, no-op on Windows).

TypeScript (`sdk/typescript/src/harness/cli.ts`):
- Add a `setInterval` idle watchdog over the existing concurrent data listeners; on idle it SIGKILLs the child and rejects with a no-progress error.
- Spawn with `stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe']` so the child's stdin receives an immediate EOF instead of an open pipe.
- Idle window resolves from the `idleSeconds` option, then env `AGENTFIELD_HARNESS_IDLE_SECONDS`, then 120s.

## Tests

Each SDK gains a test that spawns a child which prints one line then sleeps far past a short idle threshold (1s), asserting the runner aborts within seconds rather than at the wall-clock cap, plus a test that a normal fast command still returns its full output and exit code. Existing CLI tests that mocked the old wait-for-exit shape were updated to the streaming shape.

## Test plan

Python:

\`\`\`
cd sdk/python && uv run python -m pytest tests/test_run_cli_env.py tests/test_harness_cli.py tests/test_harness_runner.py tests/test_harness_provider_opencode.py tests/test_harness_provider_codex.py tests/test_harness_provider_gemini.py tests/test_harness_functional.py -q
# 86 passed
\`\`\`

Go:

\`\`\`
cd sdk/go && go build ./...        # ok
go vet ./harness/                  # no issues found
go test ./harness/ -run CLI -count=1   # ok, 7.5s
go test ./harness/ -count=1 -short     # 178 passed
\`\`\`

TypeScript:

\`\`\`
cd sdk/typescript && npx tsc --noEmit   # compilation completed
npm run build                           # ESM + DTS build success
npm test                                # 67 files, 633 tests passed
\`\`\`

The idle watchdog fires within roughly the configured window (about 1 to 2 seconds in the tests) instead of the 1800s wall-clock cap, and fast commands still return their complete output and exit code.

linked_issues.title: Harness CLI runner waits for process exit without streaming or idle watchdog, causing 30-min stalls (Python, Go, TS)
linked_issues.body:
## Summary

The CLI-based harness runner blocks until the subprocess fully exits and reads its output only at the end. It never streams the child's stdout, so it cannot apply a no-progress (idle) watchdog. When a single `opencode` call stalls (most commonly a stalled OpenRouter streaming response), the harness cannot distinguish "stuck" from "working" and waits up to the full wall-clock cap (default 1800s = 30 min), freezing the run and holding a concurrency-semaphore slot the entire time, which starves every other in-flight experiment.

This is the same flaw in all three SDKs (Python, Go, TypeScript), and the TypeScript runner additionally mishandles stdin.

## Root cause

All three runners are "wait-for-exit, read-at-end":

- Python `sdk/python/agentfield/harness/_cli.py:33-44`: `await asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=timeout)` returns only after EOF on both pipes (process exit). No streaming, no idle watchdog. `stdin` is not set, so the child inherits the parent's stdin.
- Go `sdk/go/harness/cli.go:76-80`: `c.Stdout = &stdout; c.Stderr = &stderr; err := c.Run()`. `c.Run()` is start + wait-for-exit into in-memory buffers. No streaming, no idle watchdog. `c.Stdin` is not set, so the child inherits the parent's stdin.
- TypeScript `sdk/typescript/src/harness/cli.ts:14-46`: resolves on `proc.on('close')` (wait-for-exit), accumulating stdout via `on('data')`. No completion detection from the stream. Worse, it spawns with `stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe']` and never writes to or closes `proc.stdin`, so the child's stdin is an open pipe that never receives EOF. If `opencode` reads stdin (CLIs commonly probe for piped input), it blocks forever, while Python and Go at least inherit `/dev/null` and get an immediate EOF.

The only safety net is the coarse wall-clock timeout (`AGENTFIELD_HARNESS_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`, default 1800s). Raising or lowering it is not a fix: too high freezes the run for up to 30 minutes per stall; too low kills legitimately long experiments

linked_issues.title: feat(cli): add minimal trigger surface — af call, af ls, af tail
linked_issues.body:
## Summary

Add a minimal, human-facing trigger surface to the `af` CLI so developers and scripts can invoke reasoners and watch workflows directly from the terminal. Today the server exposes `POST /api/v1/execute/:target`, `/reasoners/:id`, and `/skills/:id`, but no CLI command wraps them — the only way to call a reasoner from the shell is `af agent batch` with a hand-written JSON envelope.

This issue specifies the **complete scope**. Implementation must not exceed it.

## Scope: 3 new verbs

```
af call <node>.<reasoner>      # trigger a reasoner
af ls   [query]                # discover reasoners (recency-first, fuzzy filter)
af tail <run_id>               # attach to a running execution stream
```

No other verbs. No `af find`, `af show`, `af history`, `af repeat`, `af ctx`, `af workflow`, `af cancel`. Existing `af execution cancel|pause|resume` covers run control.

## Non-goals (explicit)

- No client-side state files, history database, or contexts file
- No per-user metrics (no "5x today" call counts)
- No TUI / interactive browser
- No pipe-chaining or batch DSL (`af agent batch` already covers programmatic batching)
- No editor-based input authoring in v1 (`--edit` flag deferred)
- No new env vars (reuse existing `AGENTFIELD_SERVER`, `AGENTFIELD_API_KEY`)

---

## `af call <node>.<reasoner>`

Triggers a reasoner. TTY-aware: prompts humans, runs silently for scripts.

### Flags

| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--in <json\|@path>` | Input payload — inline JSON string, or `@path/to/file.{json,yaml}` (curl convention, format auto-detected from extension) |
| `--schema` | Print the reasoner's input schema and exit; do not execute |
| `--async` | Return `run_id` immediately and exit; do not stream |
| `--no-interactive` | Never prompt, even on TTY (script-safe override) |
| `--interactive` | Force prompt, even when not on TTY |
| `-o pretty\|json\|yaml` | Output format. Auto: `pretty` on TTY, `json` otherwise |
| `--field <jq-path>` | Extract a single field from

title: Add cluster_labels and annotations to logical backup CronJob and Jobs
body:
## What this PR does

When using the logical backup feature, the CronJob and its created Jobs were missing the `cluster_labels` and annotations that are applied to other cluster resources like Pods, StatefulSets, and Services.

This made it difficult to filter or identify backup jobs using the same labels as other cluster components.

Fixes #1834

## Changes

- Added `ObjectMeta` with labels and annotations to `JobTemplateSpec`, so Jobs created by the CronJob inherit the cluster labels
- Updated CronJob `ObjectMeta` to use the merged labels (including `application: spilo-logical-backup`)
- Updated tests to expect the new labels

## Example

Before this fix, a logical backup CronJob would only have:
```yaml
labels:
  cluster-name: my-cluster
  team: myteam
```

After this fix:
```yaml
labels:
  application: spilo-logical-backup
  cluster-name: my-cluster
  team: myteam
  app.kubernetes.io/name: db-postgres  # custom cluster_label
```

## Type of change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

Note: parts of this implementation were developed with AI assistance.

title: Feat: enable owner references
body:
This a follow up to #2199 with some greater changes. The most important part of owner references is the cascaded removal of child resources which bypasses the delete protection offered by our operator.

Quick reminder about delete protection: this checks pre-defined annotations before calling delete code but cannot block the actual kubectl delete call itself. This you could only achieve with a K8s admission controller which, for example, does the same annotation checks.

We use the latter at Zalando, but some folks out there might rely on the protection by the operator. Therefore, we should not enable owner references by default but make it configurable.

#2199 also lacked code to update existing resources once you start using owner references or vice versa remove them. Like with the recently added annotation inheritance, this produced many code extensions. I’ve also found some oversights from #2657 which I’ve fixed along the way. All owner references syncs are done with Update API calls, which requires extending the RBAC.

A new e2e test is added which tests syncing owner references in both directions and deleting the acid-test-cluster cascadingly which was previously done in the multi namespace test. Some unit test were extended, too.

closes #2199
fixes #498

title: sync all resources to cluster fields
body:
While developing owner references support I realized once again that we store many child resources as cluster objects so we can access them even after the manifest is gone. But not for all resources, which might cause issues when deleting them because c.Namespace field might be empty:

```
time="2024-08-01T12:32:33Z" level=debug msg="removing leftover Patroni objects (endpoints / services and configmaps)" cluster-name=default/acid-clone-cluster pkg=cluster worker=2
time="2024-08-01T12:32:33Z" level=warning msg="could not fetch service \"/\": an empty namespace may not be set when a resource name is provided" cluster-name=default/acid-clone-cluster pkg=cluster worker=2
```

Fixing it for missing resources such as logical backup cron job and streams is easy. But it gets a bit tricky with objects created by Patroni, namely config service/endpoint and optionally configmaps. This PR introduces extra sync treatment for these, but only mapping to cluster fields. No diffs, no updates. This should allow us to loop over them on deletion.

Some more minor changes:
- we don't need to init the clusters kubeResources with nil
- bugfix: patched secrets and endpoints are now synced in cluster fields
- Patroni endpoints are treated separately to master/replica endpoints (only config service reused existing code, hence the extra PostgresRole)
- have only one function to determine the service/endpoint name
- small refactoring of code when services/endpoints are already deleted
- deleteSecret does not require passing the secret, we fetch it from cluster.Secrets map via uid
- if second parameter from a range loop is not needed we don't need to write an underscore (idx, _ := range -> idx := range)

The streams feature saw some bigger refactoring:
- createOrUpdateStreams was turned into syncStream for a single stream CRD which is more in line with the rest of the code
- loop over appIds is now done in syncStreams as well as the deletion of streams that got removed from the manifest (and, thus, missing in appIds list)
- renamed gatherApplicationIds function to more suitable name getDistinctApplicationIds
- deleteStream does not require passing stream, we fetch it from cluster.Streams map via appId
- the recently added check for existing slots (#2704) to allow FES creation is now comparing desired databaseSlots with actual slotsToSync. The previous comparison was not complete: appId loop with index lookup on c.Spec.Streams, which can be bigger, also with a different order.


fixes #2719

linked_issues.title: [Bug]: -enable-config-safety breaks OIDC VirtualServer configuration due to premature nginx -t validation
linked_issues.body:
### Version

edge

### What Kubernetes platforms are you running on?

Other

### Steps to reproduce

**Description**

When `-enable-config-safety` is enabled, any VirtualServer using OIDC (via `-enable-oidc`) fails to apply. The ConfigRollbackManager validates the VirtualServer config with nginx -t before the dependent OIDC config file has been written to disk, causing a guaranteed validation failure.

**Steps to Reproduce**

 1. Start NIC with `-enable-config-safety=true` and `-enable-oidc=true`
 2. Deploy an OIDC-enabled VirtualServer (e.g. examples/custom-resources/oidc)
 3. Observe that the VirtualServer configuration is never applied

**Observed Behavior**

 ```
   I20260619 03:04:55.433428 17 rollback_manager.go:71] Nginx configuration validation failed for vs_nic-examples_webapp:
     nginx: [emerg] open() "/etc/nginx/oidc-conf.d/oidc_nic-examples_webapp.conf" failed (2: No such file or directory)
     in /etc/nginx/conf.d/vs_nic-examples_webapp.conf:21
   I20260619 03:04:55.433461 17 manager.go:217] Deleting config from /etc/nginx/conf.d/vs_nic-examples_webapp.conf
 ```

 The VS config is deleted and the OIDC config is never created.

**Expected Behavior**

The VirtualServer and its OIDC configuration should be applied successfully, as they are when `-enable-config-safety` is disabled.

**Root Cause**

The write ordering in configurator.go (`addOrUpdateVirtualServer`) writes the VirtualServer config before the OIDC config:

https://github.com/nginxinc/kubernetes-ingress/blob/main/internal/configs/configurator.go#L667-L681

 ```go
   // Step 1: VS config is written and validated via nginx -t
   changed, err := cnf.nginxManager.CreateConfig(name, content)

   // Step 2: OIDC config is written (too late — step 1 already failed or deleted the VS)
   if vsCfg.Server.OIDC != nil {
       ...
       oidcChanged := cnf.nginxManager.CreateOIDCConfig(name, content)
   }
 ```

The VS config template includes:

 ```nginx
   include oidc-conf.d/oidc_<namespace>_<name>.co

title: Feat: Support Fusion API for multi-model deliberation
body:
## Summary

- add Fusion looper support for multi-model deliberation with concurrent panel calls, judge analysis, final synthesis, partial-failure handling, recursion protection, and aggregated token usage
- support direct Fusion slugs with `vllm-sr/fusion` enabled by default and `openrouter/fusion` available only as an opt-in compatibility alias
- implement Fusion-only decision matching for `model: "vllm-sr/fusion"`: requests still run signal extraction, but only decisions with `algorithm.type: fusion` are eligible; if none match, the router returns a clear no-match error instead of falling back to a normal single-model route
- add namespaced Auto aliases with `global.router.auto_model_names`; defaults are `vllm-sr/auto`, `auto`, and `MoM`, and operators can provide an explicit allow-list
- preserve the separation of concerns: `global` registers API entry aliases and looper runtime integration only; judge model, analysis panel, concurrency, token budget, templates, and error behavior remain per-decision policy under `routing.decisions[].algorithm.fusion`
- support request-level `plugins[].id == "fusion"` overrides for judge model, analysis models, concurrency, sampling, and error behavior; if no Fusion decision matches and `analysis_models` is provided, the router builds a request-scoped `fusion_direct` decision
- wire the new Fusion/Auto behavior through router config, canonical export/import, `/v1/models`, Python CLI schema/runtime mutation, dashboard config UI, docs, examples, and agent plan metadata
- fix looper internal alias routing so panel aliases route via `x-selected-model` and rewrite to provider model IDs without stale `content-length`
- harden the Kubernetes integration workflow against GitHub runner Chrome apt source mirror-sync failures

Closes #2193.

## API behavior

| Request entry | Routing behavior |
| --- | --- |
| `model: "vllm-sr/auto"` | Run full vLLM-SR signal + decision policy across all decisions. If the selected decision uses `algorithm.type: fusion`, execute Fusion; otherwise run the normal selected route. |
| `model: "auto"` / `model: "MoM"` | Legacy Auto aliases, configurable through `global.router.auto_model_names`. |
| `model: "vllm-sr/fusion"` | Run signal extraction, then evaluate Fusion-capable decisions only. No fallback to non-Fusion decisions. |
| `plugins[].id: "fusion"` | Request-scoped Fusion override for judge/panel/runtime knobs. Can provide `analysis_models` to build `fusion_direct` when no Fusion decision matches. |

## Validation

- `go test ./pkg/looper`
- `go test ./pkg/config`
- `go test ./pkg/classification`
- `go test ./pkg/extproc`
- `go test ./pkg/apiserver`
- `go test ./pkg/extproc -run 'Test(ParseFusionRequestConfig|ResolveDirectFusionDecision|ShouldUseLooper|HandleLooperInternalRequestWithPlugins)'`
- `make agent-lint CHANGED_FILES="..."`
- `make agent-ci-gate CHANGED_FILES="..."`
- `make vllm-sr-test`
- `make test-semantic-router`
- `make dashboard-check`
- post-AMD docs-only amend: `make agent-lint ENV=cpu CHANGED_FILES="docs/agent/plans/pl-0034-fusion-api.md"`
- CI workflow hardening: `make agent-validate`
- CI workflow hardening: `make agent-ci-gate CHANGED_FILES=".github/workflows/integration-test-k8s.yml"`

Notes:

- CLI integration tests stayed disabled in local fast gates because `RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS` was not set and the local Docker daemon was not reachable.
- Local dashboard Sass/Vite deprecation warnings are existing warnings; dashboard lint, type-check, unit tests, and go mod tidy all passed.

## AMD OpenRouter Regression

Validated on the AMD regression path with the router image tag `amd-regression-d5edebae-fusion`; the current PR head only adds validation notes, CI workflow hardening, and routing/config refinements after that runtime run.

The regression configured three OpenRouter model providers as Fusion backends:

- `google/gemini-3-flash-preview`
- `moonshotai/kimi-k2.6`
- `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`

Covered API paths:

- direct `model: "vllm-sr/fusion"`
- opt-in direct `model: "openrouter/fusion"` with `plugins[].id == "fusion"`
- normal Auto routing into a Fusion decision

Result summary:

| Metric | Result |
| --- | ---: |
| Fusion requests | 3 |
| Fusion requests with final assistant content | 3/3 |
| Structured analysis parses | 3/3 |
| Failed panel calls | 0 |
| Upstream Fusion model calls | 15 |
| Same-prompt single-model baseline with assistant content | 4/9 |
| Same-prompt single-model baseline reasoning-only payloads | 5/9 |

Single-model baseline average latency by backend:

| Model | Avg latency |
| --- | ---: |
| `google/gemini-3-flash-preview` | 2.64s |
| `moonshotai/kimi-k2.6` | 6.85s |
| `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` | 7.75s |

Note: the failed `integration-test (kubernetes)` run on the previous head failed before tests executed because the runner's preinstalled Google Chrome apt source was mid-sync. This PR now removes that unused apt source before `apt-get update` in the Kubernetes workflow.

linked_issues.title: Support Fusion API for multi-model deliberation
linked_issues.body:
## Summary

Add a Fusion-style API to vLLM Semantic Router for multi-model deliberation, similar to OpenRouter Fusion.

The target user experience should support two entry points:

```json
{
  "model": "vllm-sr/fusion",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "What are the strongest arguments for and against ...?"}
  ]
}
```

and request-level customization through a plugin payload:

```json
{
  "model": "vllm-sr/fusion",
  "messages": [
    {"role": "user", "content": "..."}
  ],
  "plugins": [{
    "id": "fusion",
    "model": "judge-model-name",
    "analysis_models": [
      "analysis-model-a",
      "analysis-model-b",
      "analysis-model-c"
    ]
  }]
}
```

We may also want an OpenRouter-compatible alias, for example `openrouter/fusion`, if that fits the project API policy.

## Motivation

Fusion is different from the current router selection path. It is not just selecting one best model. A Fusion-style request should:

1. fan out the prompt to multiple analysis models,
2. run those calls in parallel,
3. ask a judge/calling model to produce structured analysis across the panel outputs,
4. use that analysis to produce the final response,
5. degrade gracefully when some panel models fail.

This gives users a higher-quality deliberation mode while still exposing it through an OpenAI-compatible request shape.

Reference: https://openrouter.ai/blog/announcements/fusion-beats-frontier/

## Current state

vLLM Semantic Router already has related building blocks, but not the Fusion API or exact semantics:

- The `selection` package currently chooses one selected model for a request.
- The `looper` package is the closest execution substrate for multi-model workflows.
- `ReMoM` already implements multi-round parallel reasoning plus synthesis, so it is likely the closest starting point.
- The existing looper algorithms (`confidence`, `ratings`, `remom`, etc.) do not expose a request-level Fusion plugin API.
- A direct model slug such as `vllm-sr/fusion` or `op

## Describe the feature
Refactor the runtime packaging so `vllm-sr` becomes a router-only image while dashboard, database, and Envoy are shipped and managed as separate services/images.

## Primary layer
global level

## Why this layer?
This changes the deployment topology, image responsibilities, and inter-service contracts rather than adding a feature inside one router layer.

The current packaging mixes responsibilities that should be independently versioned and operated in production. `v0.3` should simplify the router image and make the supporting services explicit. Relevant surfaces include `src/vllm-sr/Dockerfile`, `src/vllm-sr/Dockerfile.rocm`, the CLI deploy runtime, dashboard backend/frontend services, and the Envoy sidecar/stack wiring.

title: Resolves #2062: bug: flaky memory-test-integration
body:
Closes #2062

Stops forcing the deterministic embedding bypass in CI. The memory-test-integration job now attempts to download the configured mmbert model from Hugging Face with a higher retry budget, and only falls back to deterministic embeddings if the download genuinely fails — so the tests exercise the real embedding path again.

## Changes
- `.github/workflows/integration-test-memory.yml`: `USE_DETERMINISTIC_MEMORY_EMBEDDINGS=0` and `HF_DOWNLOAD_ATTEMPTS=10` when invoking `make memory-test-integration`.
- `e2e/testing/run_memory_integration.sh`: default `USE_DETERMINISTIC_MEMORY_EMBEDDINGS` flipped from `1` to `0`; the download branch now wraps `download_hf_snapshot` in an `if` and on failure exports `VLLM_SR_DETERMINISTIC_EMBEDDINGS=1` as a safety-net fallback rather than aborting the run.

## Why this is the right layer
Modifying `embedding_deterministic.go` to make the failing tests pass would be an anti-pattern — the test queries should be served by mmbert, not by token-hash fallback. The actual regression was that PR #2044 hard-pinned the bypass on. This PR restores the intended behavior while keeping a graceful fallback for environments that genuinely can't reach HF.

## Test plan
- CI: memory-test-integration job runs with mmbert downloaded; if HF is unreachable, falls back to deterministic embeddings (with a warning) instead of failing.
- Local: `make memory-test-integration` exercises the new `if download_hf_snapshot ... else fallback` branch.

title: pkg/repo: inject chart metadata(name/version) into storage object
body:
e.g.: 0.0.1-SNAPSHOT-1 , it is a valid semver format but cannot be parsed properly (returns chart version 1 rather than 0.0.1-SNAPSHOT-1 )

Fix #220 

## Remark

I think I should add some more annotations here: 

Since deleting chart from index file should always delete the cache by chartname and chartversion , but the non-semver(or the chart not follow our parsing rules exactly) chart will be badly parsed the version with a incorrect number (as I comment in https://github.com/helm/chartmuseum/issues/220#issuecomment-669924807) . To resolve this issue , we should keep this chart version metadata within the all cache lifecycle , more closely , for the cached chart , we should add the metadata just after the cache inited (and store it in the storage.StorageObject.Metadata) , if so , the `diff` from cache and server storage will carry with the chart version , and then the `server.regenerateRepositoryIndex` will correctly regenerate the latest state of the server storage in cache.

linked_issues.title: API 响应缺少 extensions 字段 — 数据写入集群但无法通过接口读取
linked_issues.body:
## 问题描述

`SandboxCreateOptions.extensions` 的数据可以通过 API 传入并最终存储到集群（Pod annotations），但目前**没有任何 API 接口会返回 extensions 数据**。

## 数据流转分析

### ✅ 写入路径（正常工作）

1. 用户调用 `POST /sandboxes`，传入 `extensions`:
```json
{
  "extensions": {
    "opensandbox.extensions.custom-label": "中文数据"
  }
}
```

2. 服务端 `apply_extensions_to_annotations`（`server/opensandbox_server/extensions/codec.py`）将带 `opensandbox.extensions.` 前缀的 key 转换为 Pod annotation `opensandbox.io/extensions.*`

3. 最终到达集群，Pod 上有:
```yaml
metadata:
  annotations:
    opensandbox.io/extensions.custom-label: "中文数据"
```

### ❌ 读取路径（缺失）

1. **Response Schema 没有 `extensions` 字段**：
   - `CreateSandboxResponse` — 无
   - `Sandbox` — 无

2. **`_build_sandbox_from_workload`（`workload_mapper.py`）不读取 annotations 恢复 extensions**

3. 只有 `get_access_renew_extend_seconds` 单独读取 `opensandbox.io/access-renew-extend-seconds`，其他 extension 数据被忽略

## 影响范围

| 接口 | 是否返回 extensions |
|---|---|
| `POST /sandboxes` | ❌ |
| `GET /sandboxes/{id}` | ❌ |
| `GET /sandboxes` | ❌ |

## 建议修复

1. 在 `Sandbox` / `CreateSandboxResponse` schema 中添加 `extensions` 字段
2. 在 `_build_sandbox_from_workload` 中从 annotations 反向提取 `opensandbox.io/extensions.*` 并还原为 extensions dict
3. 更新 `specs/sandbox-lifecycle.yml` OpenAPI 契约

## 相关文件

- `server/opensandbox_server/api/schema.py` — response models
- `server/opensandbox_server/services/k8s/workload_mapper.py` — `_build_sandbox_from_workload`
- `server/opensandbox_server/extensions/codec.py` — `apply_extensions_to_annotations`
- `specs/sandbox-lifecycle.yml` — API 契约

linked_issues.title: Make the egress sidecar work under `runtimeClassName: gvisor` (or document the incompatibility)
linked_issues.body:
## Why do you need it?

The two features I most wanted to combine in an AI-sandbox deployment are the **per-sandbox FQDN egress control** (OSEP-0001 / `docs/network-isolation-for-kubernetes.md`) and **gVisor as the secure container runtime** (OSEP-0004 / `docs/secure-container.md`). The project documents both as if they compose cleanly — secure_runtime is described in OSEP-0004 as a "transparent infrastructure upgrade" requiring no SDK changes, and the egress sidecar is described as the primary network-isolation primitive injected automatically by `apply_egress_to_spec` whenever a `network_policy` is set. In practice the two cannot be enabled together today: the egress sidecar fails to start under gVisor because gVisor's netstack does not implement the iptables `nat` table that the sidecar's DNS redirect depends on. From an operator's perspective this is the most natural setup ("strong syscall isolation **and** strong network isolation, because the workload is a compromised-AI threat model"), and it's the one that doesn't work.

What I would like, in order of preference:

1. The egress sidecar transparently supports gVisor (e.g. by switching the DNS interception from REDIRECT to a `mangle`-table-based TPROXY divert, which gVisor does support).
2. Failing that, the server validates the combination at startup and returns a clean error to the operator instead of producing CrashLoopBackOff pods.
3. At a minimum, the compatibility matrix in `docs/secure-container.md` adds a row for "iptables `nat` table / egress sidecar" with explicit "No" under gVisor and "Use Kata instead" guidance in the troubleshooting section — same shape as the resolution given in #570.

## How could it be?

### Concrete behaviour I'd want

Either of these would solve my use case:

- **`secure_runtime.type = "gvisor"` + `network_policy` works end-to-end.** I create a sandbox via the SDK with a `network_policy`, the server injects the egress sidecar via `apply_egress_to_spec` as it does today, and t

title: Rework runner job submission flow
body:
The overall process is more lenient now:

* It's safe to call `/api/submit` more than once -- even after `/api/upload_code`
* It's safe to call `/api/upload_code` more than once or don't call it at all -- code upload is now optional -- if we have nothing to upload, we can skip this step

Since `/api/upload_code` is now optional, the server no longer calls this method if there is no code (`RemoteRepo` diff or `VirtualRepo` file archive) to upload. In addition, Python API (used by CLI internally) has been optimized -- it does not upload a code blob in there is no diff (`RemoteRepo`) or no files (empty `VirtualRepo`, incl. default virtual repo used when no repo specified in the run configuration).

Fixes: https://github.com/dstackai/dstack/issues/3740

linked_issues.title: [Bug]: `_submit_job_to_runner` is unrecoverably broken once any runner API call after `/api/submit` failed
linked_issues.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Build `dstack-runner` with the following patch:

```patch
diff --git runner/internal/runner/api/http.go runner/internal/runner/api/http.go
index 34220acc6..dfd9db99c 100644
--- runner/internal/runner/api/http.go
+++ runner/internal/runner/api/http.go
@@ -130,6 +130,11 @@ func (s *Server) uploadCodePostHandler(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) (
 		return nil, &api.Error{Status: http.StatusConflict}
 	}

+	if !s.uploadCodeCalledOnce {
+		s.uploadCodeCalledOnce = true
+		return nil, &api.Error{Status: http.StatusInternalServerError}
+	}
+
 	r.Body = http.MaxBytesReader(w, r.Body, maxBodySize)

 	if err := s.executor.WriteRepoBlob(r.Body); err != nil {
diff --git runner/internal/runner/api/server.go runner/internal/runner/api/server.go
index 11b76d887..4872f38ef 100644
--- runner/internal/runner/api/server.go
+++ runner/internal/runner/api/server.go
@@ -27,6 +27,8 @@ type Server struct {
 	executor  executor.Executor
 	cancelRun context.CancelFunc

+	uploadCodeCalledOnce bool
+
 	metricsCollector *metrics.MetricsCollector

 	version string
```

It emulates a flaky failure of `/api/upload_code` handler (e.g., a network issue) – the first call fails, all consecutive calls succeed.

Deploy this build and submit a run as usual.

### Actual behaviour

Once `_submit_job_to_runner` fails in any runner's API call other than `/api/submit`, all consecutive attempts are deemed to fail as the previous attempt changed runner's executor state to `WaitCode`|`WaitRun` and `/api/submit` rejects submission since the state is not `WaitSubmit`.

If we ignore 409 in `/api/submit` call, the submission process recovers:

```patch
--- src/dstack/_internal/server/background/pipeline_tasks/jobs_running.py
+++ src/dstack/_internal/server/background/pipeline_tasks/jobs_running.py
@@ -1342,16 +1342,19 @@ def _submit_job_to_runner(
     if runner_client.healthcheck() is None:
         return _SubmitJobToRunnerResult(success=success_if_not_available)

-    runner_client

title: Fix: ValueError: too many values to unpack in list_tenant_added_models for model IDs containing '@' (#16467)
body:
## Summary

Fixes #16467 — `ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected 3)` in `list_tenant_added_models` for model IDs containing `@`, **and three sibling code paths that exhibit the same `@`-mis-parsing bug**. Adds a downstream `NotFoundError` tolerance so the dataset-configuration "check embedding" endpoint degrades to a user-friendly message instead of HTTP 500 when the tenant chunk index is missing.

### Root cause (recap)

The composite model key is right-anchored:

```
model_name@instance_name@provider_name      # explicit instance
model_name@provider_name                    # default instance
```

Some model names legitimately contain `@` characters (LM Studio embedding model IDs like `text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5@q8_0`, llama.cpp `@<quantization>` suffixes, HuggingFace dataset IDs). The full composite key then has **4+** `@`-separated fields:

```
text-embedding-nomic-embed-text-v1.5@q8_0@lmstudio@LM-Studio
```

A naive `split("@")` + 3-element unpack mis-assigns the fields.

### Sites fixed

| # | File | Function | Fix |
|---|------|----------|-----|
| 1 | `api/apps/services/models_api_service.py` | `_get_model_info` | `key.rsplit("@", 2)`; bare-name case sets empty provider |
| 2 | `api/db/joint_services/tenant_model_service.py` | `split_model_name` | `rsplit("@", 2)` from the right |
| 3 | `internal/service/model_service.go` | `parseModelName` | Rejoins leading fields when >3 `@`-segments |
| 4 | `internal/service/model_service.go` | `GetModelConfigFromProviderInstance` | TEI exact-match + Builtin suffix-based detection to handle bare model IDs with embedded `@` |
| 5 | `api/apps/services/models_api_service.py` | `list_tenant_added_models` | Internal map keys use `|` separator instead of `@` to avoid conflict with model names containing `@` |
| 6 | `api/apps/services/dataset_api_service.py` | `sample_random_chunks_with_vectors` | Graceful empty-set return on missing chunk index |

### Review feedback addressed (commit 26a2cec59)

- **Removed deprecated `TenantLLMService` dependency** from `models_api_service.py` — the `_resolve_bare_model_provider()` function and its `TenantLLMService` import were deleted per maintainer request.
- **Replaced `@` with `|` as internal map key separator** in `list_tenant_added_models` to avoid conflicts with model names containing `@`.
- **Fixed Go TEI/Builtin fast paths** — the TEI short-circuit now does exact string match first to handle bare model IDs with embedded `@`. The Builtin provider detection uses `strings.HasSuffix` instead of `splitRightAnchoredModelName` to avoid misidentifying embedded `@` as a provider separator.
- **Updated tests** — removed bare-model tests that depended on deprecated `TenantLLMService`, added test for two-part model IDs with embedded `@`.

### Testing

- **Python**: 5/5 unit tests pass (`test_models_api_service_list_tenant_added_models.py`)
- **Go**: `gofmt` validates syntax; Go tests require Linux (platform-specific `binding` package)
- **Regression tests**: `test_tenant_model_service_split_model_name.py` and `model_service_test.go` cover the parsing fixes

title: cli: add --skip flag to dagger check
body:
## What

Adds a `--skip` flag to `dagger check` to exclude checks matching a pattern — the inverse of the existing inclusive pattern arguments:

```console
dagger check --skip '**e2e'        # run all checks except those matching **e2e
dagger check -l --skip 'failing-*' # preview what would run
dagger check go --skip 'go:lint'   # combines with include patterns
```

## How

- `Workspace.checks` gains a `skip: [String!]` argument. Skip patterns are applied with the same `filterNodesByExclude` helper already used for config-driven `[check] skip` patterns, so matching semantics are identical to the inclusive patterns (doublestar glob + path prefix + single-module compat fallback), just inverted. Include and skip compose: a check runs if it matches the include patterns and does not match any skip pattern.
- The CLI flag is repeatable and applies to both running and listing (`-l`).
- Past Cloud Checks results can't be filtered by skip patterns, so `--skip` always runs checks live: it bypasses the past-results replay and is mutually exclusive with `--past`.

## Testing

- New `TestChecksSkipFlag` integration tests covering list/run with `--skip`, glob (`**:unit`) and prefix (`test`) patterns, include+skip combined, and `--past`/`--skip` exclusivity.
- Verified end-to-end against a dev engine built from this branch, including include/skip parity: for every pattern form tested, whatever include matches, skip excludes.

Closes #13335
